intro: Eriosorus caracasanus, also known as Caracasan or Caracasan sedge, is a type of plant species belonging to the family of flowering plants known as the Cyperaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriosorus caracasanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriosorus caracasanus is often prepared in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments.
